Content: Schafer: Hi, guys. How's it going?
Content_zh: Schafer：大家好，最近怎麼樣？

Content: This is the last one of these we'll ever do?
Content_zh: 這是我們最後一次做這個了嗎？

Content: Offscreen: Yeah.
Content_zh: 畫外音：是啊。

Content: Schafer: Seems like only yesterday I was saying all that stuff, sitting in this chair
Content_zh: Schafer：感覺才像昨天，我還坐在這張椅子上講那些話，

Content: that I can't remember now, 'cause it's been so long ago.
Content_zh: 但我現在都不記得了，因為已經太久了。

Content: Offscreen: Mm-hmm, we talked about that initial budget in that chair.
Content_zh: 畫外音：嗯哼，我們就是在那張椅子上聊到最初的預算。

Content: Schafer: Don't bring that up.
Content_zh: Schafer：別提那個。

Content: [Laughter] Yeah, it's just-- it's this one,
Content_zh: [笑聲] 對，就是……就是這個，

Content: and then it's, ahh!
Content_zh: 然後就是，啊！

Content: [Music] Schafer: Tonight, we are going to have a bonfire down at the beach
Content_zh: [音樂] Schafer：今晚我們要到海灘生營火，

Content: to celebrate the completion of "Broken Age," everybody.
Content_zh: 慶祝《Broken Age》完成了，各位。

Content: [Applause] Yay!
Content_zh: [掌聲] 耶！

Content: Teamwork!
Content_zh: 團隊合作！

Content: It started out like a really fun idea, and then it got really hard.
Content_zh: 一開始是個很有趣的點子，後來就變得超難。

Content: And a bunch of people wussed out, but a lot of people stuck around to the end.
Content_zh: 有一堆人撐不下去，但很多人一路撐到了最後。

Content: I wanna thank you, thank you to the whole team, and thank you everybody.
Content_zh: 我想謝謝大家，謝謝整個團隊，也謝謝所有人。

Content: We're done!
Content_zh: 我們完成了！

Content: Like, we went to the beach, we had that party, things got a little out of control.
Content_zh: 我們去了海灘，辦了派對，場面有點失控。

Content: [Yelling] And it's fun, it's fun 'cause it's, like,
Content_zh: [叫喊聲] 這很好玩，因為就像……

Content: not fixing things right at the last minute.
Content_zh: 不是到最後一刻才在修東西。

Content: We kinda had it done in advance a little bit, at least for-- for me and us.
Content_zh: 我們算是有提前一點完成，至少對我、對我們來說是這樣。

Content: Like, it was, um, wrapped and submitted.
Content_zh: 就是，嗯，已經封版並送審了。

Content: Like, we had to submit for the disc version, and that kind of actually-- it was tough,
Content_zh: 我們得送出光碟版，那其實……那很辛苦，

Content: but it forced us to have a lot of stuff done before the, um, actual launch date,
Content_zh: 但也逼我們在真正的發行日前先完成很多東西，

Content: 'cause we had to have that version burnt to disc.
Content_zh: 因為我們得把那個版本燒成光碟。

Content: And so, um...we had to time to kind of, like...
Content_zh: 所以，嗯……我們有時間稍微……

Content: adjust to the thought of it being over.
Content_zh: 適應它真的結束了這件事。

Content: Um, so, Reds is done and it's awesome.
Content_zh: 嗯，所以 Reds 完成了，而且超棒。

Content: The end.
Content_zh: 全劇終。

Content: Rice: It's done?
Content_zh: Rice：搞定了？

Content: Oh my God.
Content_zh: 我的天啊。

Content: That's kinda sad.
Content_zh: 這還滿令人難過的。

Content: We sent review keys out to press for "Broken Age," so there's already, like, 10 or so folks
Content_zh: 我們已經把《Broken Age》的評測序號寄給媒體了，所以已經有大概十個人

Content: that are playing for review.
Content_zh: 正在玩評測版。

Content: With the amount of time that it's taken and the amount of things that have happened
Content_zh: 花了很多時間 發生了很多事

Content: in the last three years is, uh, staggering.
Content_zh: 過去三年發生了這麼多事，真的很驚人。

Content: And, um, it was nice to see everyone care enough to push for a final product
Content_zh: 而且，嗯，看到大家都這麼在乎，願意努力把成品推到最後，

Content: that could be something we're proud of.
Content_zh: 做成一個我們能引以為傲的東西，感覺很好。

Content: Crook: Um, so yeah, it just, um, it kind of all came together at the end.
Content_zh: Crook：嗯，所以對，最後一切算是都兜起來了。

Content: Um...so I feel good right now.
Content_zh: 嗯……所以我現在感覺很好。

Content: Offscreen: You feel good about it? Crook: Yeah.
Content_zh: 畫外音：你對它感覺不錯？Crook：對。

Content: I think it's beautiful, and something that we should be proud of.
Content_zh: 我覺得這很美 我們應該以之為榮

Content: Uh, I think people are gonna really like it.
Content_zh: 我想人們會很喜歡的

Content: Russell: Yes, we're finished.
Content_zh: Russell：對，我們完成了。

Content: See, I knew it would happen.
Content_zh: 我就知道會這樣

Content: And after 13, 14 years here, you know stuff's gonna get done,
Content_zh: 在這裡待了十三、十四年之後，你就知道事情總會做完，

Content: and it'll be great in the end.
Content_zh: 最後會很棒的

Content: It'll be all worth it.
Content_zh: 一切都會值得的。

Content: Schafer: Rusty. Russell: I'm playin' through and fixin' bugs.
Content_zh: Schafer：Rusty。Russell：我在玩一輪，順便修 bug。

Content: Schafer: Well, thanks for playing.
Content_zh: Schafer：好，謝謝你玩。

Content: Russell: Mostly playing. Schafer: Mm-hmm.
Content_zh: Russell：主要是在玩。Schafer：嗯哼。

Content: Awesome. What do you think so far?
Content_zh: 太棒了 你覺得怎麼樣？

Content: Russell: I got confused, actually.
Content_zh: Russell：其實我有點搞混了。

Content: As much as I played through-- -I was-- Schafer: That's not good!
Content_zh: 我玩到……我當時……Schafer：那可不妙！

Content: Rice: It's not a scripted cutscene, so you wouldn't know.
Content_zh: Rice：那不是照劇本跑的過場動畫，所以你不會知道。

Content: Russell: Yeah, exactly.
Content_zh: Russell：沒錯

Content: Schafer: Excellent, did you figure it out, or did ask for a hint?
Content_zh: Schafer：太好了，你有自己解出來，還是去問提示？

Content: Russell: I had to ask Ray.
Content_zh: Russell：我只好去問 Ray。

Content: Schafer: Oh... -Oh...
Content_zh: Schafer：哦……

Content: And also, look, it's running on, uh, iPhone 6.
Content_zh: 而且你看，它跑在 iPhone 6 上。

Content: -Thanks... Rice: Not anymore.
Content_zh: ——謝啦……Rice：現在沒了。

Content: Schafer: Not--b-but--it's a black screen.
Content_zh: Schafer：不……可、可是……它變黑畫面了。

Content: Oliver!
Content_zh: 奧利弗！

Content: Oh, spoilers for "Act 2"!
Content_zh: 喔，《第二幕》劇透！

Content: Okay.
Content_zh: 好吧

Content: Anyway, it's running.
Content_zh: 總之，它跑得起來。

Content: Franzke: But, yeah.
Content_zh: Franzke：不過，對。

Content: As you can see, the game is running a little thing-- This one just crashed, but it does work.
Content_zh: 你們可以看到，遊戲有在跑……這台剛當掉了，但它真的能跑。

Content: I am extremely proud of, you know, the product the way it is right now.
Content_zh: 我對現在這個成品非常驕傲。

Content: Um...and, yeah, I hope it will stand the test of time.
Content_zh: 嗯…… 是的 我希望它能耐得住時間的考驗

Content: Schafer: And then we are on the street on April 28th.
Content_zh: Schafer：然後我們 4 月 28 日就要上市了。

Content: It'll be awesome.
Content_zh: 會很棒的

Content: Um, and Anna's alive.
Content_zh: 安娜還活著

Content: Look at that, everybody.
Content_zh: 大家看

Content: Yay, Anna's alive!
Content_zh: 耶，安娜還活著！

Content: Yay!
Content_zh: 耶！

Content: Kipnis: It feels--it feels good.
Content_zh: Kipnis：感覺……感覺很好。

Content: It feels like, uh, we did-- we did something good and worthwhile
Content_zh: 感覺好像我們做了…… 我們做了一件值得做的事

Content: and something that hopefully will, you know, uh, have a-- I don't know, a future?
Content_zh: 希望它能，嗯……我也不知道，能有個未來？

Content: Like, have a legacy of some kind.
Content_zh: 像是留下某種傳承。

Content: Schafer: I really, uh, felt good about the second chapter.
Content_zh: Schafer：我真的，嗯，對第二章感覺很好。

Content: You know, I feel like it answered anyone's critique about the first one,
Content_zh: 我覺得它回應了大家對第一章的批評，

Content: you know, without changing the game completely, you know.
Content_zh: 而且沒有把遊戲整個改掉。

Content: I think it definitely was, you know, harder and longer, and, um, and amazingly done in less time, so...
Content_zh: 我覺得它確實更難、更長，而且很神奇地用更少時間完成了，所以……

Content: Stoddard: I did my first full, full playthrough last weekend, and it is a hard game.
Content_zh: Stoddard：我上週末第一次完整跑完一輪，這遊戲真的很難。

Content: It's really challenging and it's really well-done and it's engaging.
Content_zh: 它真的很有挑戰性，完成度很高，也很吸引人。

Content: Like, I've worked on this thing for years and I still-- like, the story was still getting me.
Content_zh: 我做這東西做了好幾年，結果還是……故事還是打動了我。

Content: It's pretty good.
Content_zh: 它真的不錯。

Content: And when I hit the final scene, like, man, dude,
Content_zh: 當我玩到最後一幕時，天啊，老兄，

Content: it's, uh... it's cinematic.
Content_zh: 那個，呃……很有電影感。

Content: It's like it hits you.
Content_zh: 就像會打中你。

Content: Definitely.
Content_zh: 當然

Content: Um...so that felt good.
Content_zh: 嗯…… 感覺很好

Content: I just had to have a moment to...
Content_zh: 我只是需要一點時間……

Content: start loving it again.
Content_zh: 又開始喜歡它了

Content: I mean, like, I had this point in here where, like, the love I have for this just kinda
Content_zh: 我是說，有一段時間，我對這東西的愛好像

Content: got knocked out for a few days.
Content_zh: 消失了好幾天。

Content: And that was really scary.
Content_zh: 那真的很可怕

Content: That was really scary.
Content_zh: 真可怕

Content: And, um...
It was hard.
Content_zh: 很難

Content: Schafer: We're going to our last stand-ups?
Content_zh: Schafer：我們要去最後一次站會了嗎？

Content: Is it last stand-ups?
Content_zh: 這是最後一次站會嗎？

Content: I believe it is.
Content_zh: 我相信

Content: Hello, everybody.
Content_zh: 大家好

Content: Oh, look at the big-- Did we not get bagels for the final stand-ups?
Content_zh: 喔，看看那個大……我們最後一次站會沒有準備貝果嗎？

Content: Crook: Ah, I didn't try.
Content_zh: Crook：我沒試過

Content: Schafer: Come on! It's our last one.
Content_zh: Schafer：拜託！這可是最後一次了。

Content: Get your ass off Bagel.
Content_zh: Bagel，快動起來。

Content: I brought this for emotional support.
Content_zh: 我帶這個是為了情感支持

Content: In case I get emotional.
Content_zh: 以防我情緒失控。

Content: Crook: Just keep hugging it. Schafer: I'm not gonna get emotional.
Content_zh: Crook：就一直抱著它。Schafer：我才不會情緒失控。

Content: Crook: Squeezing it, hugging it. Schafer: I doubt it.
Content_zh: Crook：捏它、抱它。Schafer：我才不信。

Content: I doubt it. We're not done.
Content_zh: 我才不信。我們還沒結束。

Content: Are we? We done?
Content_zh: 是嗎？我們結束了？

Content: Crook: This is the last standup.
Content_zh: Crook：這是最後一次站會。

Content: Schafer: You playing right now?
Content_zh: Schafer：你在玩嗎？

Content: This game is so addictive.
Content_zh: 這遊戲太上癮了

Content: Cannot put it down for one second.
Content_zh: 一秒都放不下來。

Content: Um...this might be-- this is our last proper standup for the full team, but, uh...
Content_zh: 嗯……這可能是……這是整個團隊最後一次正式站會，不過，呃……

Content: don't worry, I won't give a speech or anything.
Content_zh: 別擔心 我不會發言的

Content: And then you always have this post-project depression, which is, like, you've defined yourself by who you are
Content_zh: 然後你總是會有這種專案結束後的憂鬱，就像你做這款遊戲做了這麼久，

Content: by making this game for so long, and then it's over and you're like, "Who am I?
Content_zh: 已經用這件事定義自己，結果它結束了，你就會想：「我是誰？

Content: Am I not the person who makes 'Broken Age'?
Content_zh: 我難道不是那個做《Broken Age》的人嗎？

Content: What am I? What's the point of it all?" Crook: Before, when we were just doing, like, a big game,
Content_zh: 我是什麼？這一切有什麼意義？」Crook：以前我們做大型遊戲時，

Content: it would ship, and then the whole studio didn't have anything to do for a while.
Content_zh: 遊戲一推出，整個工作室就會有一段時間沒事做。

Content: And every--I think every-- the whole-- You could kinda feel that in the air
Content_zh: 然後每個……我覺得每個……整個……你可以感覺到那種氣氛，

Content: and it just kind of-- it was just-- it was kind of a downer.
Content_zh: 然後就有點……就是……有點低落。

Content: And, uh, because there's multiple projects now, we can all just jump onto things,
Content_zh: 因為現在有多個專案，我們都可以直接跳去做別的事，

Content: and that helps-- helps you, uh, I think helps me, get out of that.
Content_zh: 這會幫助你，嗯，我想也幫助我走出來。

Content: And so that's why there was a need on "Day of the Tentacle."
Content_zh: 所以《Day of the Tentacle》那邊才會需要人。

Content: Um, so now-- now I'm on to that.
Content_zh: 嗯，所以現在……現在我就去做那個了。

Content: Um, I'm--I hear it's really bad for people who are leading the project.
Content_zh: 嗯，我……我聽說對帶專案的人來說會很難受。

Content: Uh, but Tim doesn't-- I mean, he doesn't show it.
Content_zh: 呃，但 Tim 不會……我是說，他不會表現出來。

Content: He's pretty good with hiding, I think, his sadness.
Content_zh: 他很擅長隱藏 他的悲傷

Content: But, um...
Content_zh: 但是……

Content: He's just sad all the time.
Content_zh: 他總是很傷心

Content: No.
Content_zh: 不

Content: Uh, but I'm sure he-- I'm sure it will hit him, you know,
Content_zh: 呃，但我確定他……我確定他遲早會有感覺，

Content: because this is three years, uh, put into this.
Content_zh: 因為這可是投入了三年的時間。

Content: Schafer: No, I don't get depressed.
Content_zh: Schafer：不，我不會憂鬱。

Content: I'm too old to get depressed.
Content_zh: 我年紀太大了，憂鬱不起來。

Content: Offscreen: Don't you have any sympathy for all the people that have been insisting the game will never finish?
Content_zh: 畫外音：那些一直堅稱這遊戲永遠做不完的人，你都不同情他們嗎？

Content: Schafer: I do--I do feel bad for all the people who have based their identity
Content_zh: Schafer：我會……我確實替那些把自我認同建立在

Content: on the fact that I'll never finish this game.
Content_zh: 「我永遠做不完這遊戲」這件事上的人感到難過。

Content: 'Cause we'll finish it and then they'll have post-project depression.
Content_zh: 因為我們會把它完成，然後他們就會有專案結束後的憂鬱。

Content: If I'm not that person who hates Tim Schafer, who am I?
Content_zh: 如果我不是那個討厭 Tim Schafer 的人，那我是誰？

Content: Excellent.
Content_zh: 很好

Content: Thank you. Panya's last standup, for sure.
Content_zh: 謝謝。Panya 的最後一次站會，這次肯定是了。

Content: Inversin: Yeah, for sure.
Content_zh: Inversin：對，肯定是。

Content: I'm out this week, guys, so it's been a pleasure.
Content_zh: 我這週就要離開了，各位，很高興能跟大家共事。

Content: But, uh, uh, yeah.
Content_zh: 不過，呃，嗯，對。

Content: Crook: Go inside, Victor. You're next.
Content_zh: Crook：進去吧，Victor。下一個換你。

Content: Schafer: Yeah, nice try, Victor. Crook: There you go.
Content_zh: Schafer：對，想得美，Victor。Crook：好了。

Content: Romero: Almost. -Schafer: All right, we'll miss you, Panya. Victor!
Content_zh: Romero：差一點。Schafer：好吧，我們會想你的，Panya。Victor！

Content: Crook: Yep. Schafer: Victor.
Content_zh: Crook：對。Schafer：Victor。

Content: Romero: I'll miss you, Panya, uh...
Content_zh: Romero：我會想你的 潘雅 呃……

Content: Schafer: Oh, shipping games is always tense, and then afterwards there's this moment
Content_zh: Schafer：推出遊戲總是很緊繃，之後就會有一個時刻，

Content: where if there's ever gonna be any turnaround in the company,
Content_zh: 如果公司會有人事變動，

Content: it usually happens at that moment, 'cause people hang on to the--they want--
Content_zh: 通常就會發生在那時候，因為大家都撐著……他們想……

Content: everyone wants to finish the project they're on.
Content_zh: 每個人都想完成自己手上的專案。

Content: That--so that's usually the period where people will come and go,
Content_zh: 所以那通常就是人員來來去去的時期，

Content: is--is in that period.
Content_zh: 就是在那段時間。

Content: Okay, everyone, thanks for coming to dailies all those times.
Content_zh: 好了各位，謝謝你們這麼多次都來參加每日會議。

Content: It's over. Next project.
Content_zh: 結束了 下一個專案

Content: Rice: Yay! Crook: That's it.
Content_zh: Rice：耶！Crook：就這樣。

Content: Stoddard: Man, he's sentimental.
Content_zh: Stoddard：天啊，他真感性。

Content: Crook: Yeah.
Content_zh: Crook：是啊。

Content: Coull: So.
Content_zh: Coull：所以……

Content: Sad panda- I'm leaving.
Content_zh: 難過熊貓——我要走了。

Content: Um...
What else do you want to know about it.
Content_zh: 你們還想知道什麼？

Content: No, I mean, um, the contract was supposed to come to an end anyway.
Content_zh: 不是，我是說，嗯，合約本來就預定要結束了。

Content: The fact that it went on a month after was pretty-- pretty awesome for me.
Content_zh: 它後來又多延了一個月，對我來說其實……其實很棒。

Content: You know, maybe they'll need me again in the future, but, you know, you can't be certain, I guess.
Content_zh: 也許他們未來還會需要我，但你知道，誰也說不準。

Content: Schafer: Oliver is still cranking away on the final final versions of it
Content_zh: Schafer：Oliver 還在努力處理最後、最後的版本，

Content: to get it out the door.
Content_zh: 好把它推出去。

Content: And then Oliver will be moving back to Germany, sadly.
Content_zh: 然後 Oliver 很遺憾地要搬回德國。

Content: I think some of you know this; we've talked about this.
Content_zh: 我想你們有些人知道 我們談過了

Content: Oliver is--the-- the--the pull of the fatherland is too strong.
Content_zh: Oliver 是……那個……祖國的召喚太強了。

Content: He must go back.
Content_zh: 他必須回去

Content: Oliver wants to move.
Content_zh: 奧利弗想搬家

Content: He's gonna keep working for us, but he's gonna move to Germany.
Content_zh: 他會繼續為我們工作 但他會搬到德國去

Content: But he's gonna be working on "Day of the Tentacle." So you don't have to be mean to Oliver.
Content_zh: 但他會做《Day of the Tentacle》。所以你們不用對 Oliver 太刻薄。

Content: That's the main point. Usually when someone's leaving, we make sure everyone knows in advance
Content_zh: 這才是重點。通常有人要離開時，我們都會確保大家事先知道，

Content: so that they can be mean to them.
Content_zh: 這樣大家才可以對他刻薄。

Content: Franzke: So in a few weeks, I'm actually going to move back.
Content_zh: Franzke：所以幾週後，我真的要搬回去了。

Content: So it will be interesting to see how...
Content_zh: 到時候看看……

Content: how much German culture has changed since I moved away.
Content_zh: 自從我搬走後 德國文化改變了多少

Content: Maybe I'll feel like a complete foreigner for a while.
Content_zh: 也許有一陣子我會覺得自己完全像個外國人。

Content: Who knows? I don't even know.
Content_zh: 誰知道呢？ 我都不知道

Content: So, all future interviews will have to be through Skype.
Content_zh: 所以以後所有訪談都得透過 Skype 了。

Content: Offscreen: Gonna miss you, Oliver. Franzke: Yeah...
Content_zh: 畫外音：會想你的，Oliver。Franzke：對……

Content: I'm gonna miss you guys too.
Content_zh: 我也會想念你們的

Content: It's gonna be weird.
Content_zh: 會很奇怪的

Content: Schafer: I think it's-- it's been a tough time because we haven't, you know,
Content_zh: Schafer：我覺得……這段時間很辛苦，因為我們，呃，

Content: it's been a while since we've given raises to people, and so there's other companies in the area
Content_zh: 已經有一陣子沒幫大家加薪了，而這一帶有其他公司

Content: that are making a lot of money right now, and so they're able to throw that kind of money around,
Content_zh: 現在賺很多錢，所以他們能到處砸那種錢，

Content: and that'll probably cause us to-- Uh, that always happens.
Content_zh: 那可能會讓我們…… 總是這樣

Content: We lose through--when, like, Oculus and Facebook took off, they started throwing money around.
Content_zh: 我們會流失人手……像 Oculus 和 Facebook 起飛時，他們就開始到處砸錢。

Content: We lost some people to that.
Content_zh: 我們失去了一些人

Content: Um, but after "Broken Age" comes out, we'll be able to throw money around, right,
Content_zh: 嗯，不過等《Broken Age》推出後，我們也能到處砸錢了，對吧，

Content: 'cause we'll be rich off of, uh, all those point-and-click adventure dollars.
Content_zh: 因為我們會靠那些點擊式冒險遊戲的錢發大財。

Content: Actually, we don't expect to make a lot of money off of "Broken Age,"
Content_zh: 其實我們不期待《Broken Age》能賺很多錢，

Content: because everyone bought it the first time.
Content_zh: 因為大家第一次就已經買過了。

Content: And, um, but the new platforms like, um, PS4 are going to be opening up some new customers.
Content_zh: 不過像 PS4 這類新平台會帶來一些新客群。

Content: Bailey: There's a lot banking on it, but--but not everything.
Content_zh: Bailey：我們在它身上押了不少，但……但不是全部。

Content: I mean, we diversified to do some of those LucasArts properties
Content_zh: 我是說，我們也分散去做了一些 LucasArts 作品，

Content: like "Grim Fandango" and, like, uh, "Day of the Tentacle." Uh, so "Grim" sales are really good.
Content_zh: 像《Grim Fandango》，還有，呃，《Day of the Tentacle》。嗯，所以《Grim》的銷量真的很好。

Content: Um, you know...
Content_zh: 嗯，你知道……

Content: [Applause] It's, like, $1.7 million gross,
Content_zh: [掌聲] 總營收大概是一百七十萬美元，

Content: which is good.
Content_zh: 很好

Content: So, across all platforms for the last week, um...
Content_zh: 所以，上週所有平台加起來，嗯……

Content: Schafer: I'm feelin' kinda gross. Bailey: Yeah.
Content_zh: Schafer：我覺得有點 gross。Bailey：對。

Content: And, you know, it looks like those are-- are gonna bring in a lot of, uh,
Content_zh: 而且，你知道，看起來那些……會帶來很多，呃，

Content: a lot of money for the studio, so...
Content_zh: 很多錢給工作室，所以……

Content: Schafer: And I hope there's a lot of people out there who were just waiting for the whole thing to be one game
Content_zh: Schafer：我也希望外面有很多人只是一直等著整個東西變成一款完整遊戲，

Content: and don't care about crowdfunding and they don't wanna know about
Content_zh: 不在乎群眾募資，也不想知道

Content: our little transparency efforts.
Content_zh: 我們那些小小的透明化努力。

Content: They just wanna play a game when it's done and, um, hopefully they will buy it.
Content_zh: 他們只是想在遊戲完成後玩它，嗯，希望他們會買。

Content: Please join me in welcoming Mr. James Spafford.
Content_zh: 請跟我一起歡迎 James Spafford 先生。

Content: -Yay! -Thank you, guys.
Content_zh: -耶！ -謝謝你們

Content: James Spafford.
Content_zh: James Spafford。

Content: Uh, would you please stand up so everyone can see you.
Content_zh: 請站起來讓大家看到你

Content: This is James, and rotate one time.
Content_zh: 這位是 James，請轉一圈。

Content: No, you don't have to rotate.
Content_zh: 不，你不用真的轉。

Content: Uh, James...
Content_zh: James……

Content: uh, welcome to Double Fine.
Content_zh: 歡迎來到Double Fine

Content: So James Spafford used to be one of the two people who ran Mixnmojo, which is an old LucasArts fan site,
Content_zh: James Spafford 以前是 Mixnmojo 兩位經營者之一，那是個老牌 LucasArts 粉絲網站，

Content: and I knew him back then when he visited our offices in 2001, I think, and gave me some Marmite.
Content_zh: 那時候他 2001 年來我們辦公室，還送我一些 Marmite，我想就是那時候認識他的。

Content: And, um...and now I said, "In 10 years, we'll hire you."
Content_zh: 然後……我現在就說：「十年後，我們會雇用你。」

Content: Fifteen years? "In 15 years, we'll hire you"-- as our community manager.
Content_zh: 十五年？「十五年後，我們會雇用你」——當我們的社群經理。

Content: -Whoa. -He's not the new Chris Remo.
Content_zh: ——哇。——他不是新的 Chris Remo。

Content: He will never be the new Chris Remo!
Content_zh: 他永遠不會是新的 Chris Remo！

Content: -Yeah. Muir: 'Cause he's not dead.
Content_zh: ——對。Muir：因為他又還沒死。

Content: Schafer: In other news, Chris Remo's dead.
Content_zh: Schafer：順帶一提，Chris Remo 死了。

Content: No.
Content_zh: 不

Content: Spafford: I mean, I'm a long-time follower of Double Fine and everything they've been doing.
Content_zh: Spafford：我是說，我一直都有在關注 Double Fine，還有他們做的所有事情。

Content: I'm a backer.
Content_zh: 我是贊助者

Content: Uh, obviously backed immediately.
Content_zh: 呃，當然是一開始就贊助了。

Content: Um, followed the documentary series, which I'm now on, which is really weird, uh...
Content_zh: 也一直看這部紀錄片系列，現在我居然在裡面，感覺真的很怪，呃……

Content: Offscreen: Is he aware of everything that's happened?
Content_zh: 畫外音：他知道發生的一切嗎？

Content: Did he know what he was getting himself into?
Content_zh: 他知道自己在搞什麼嗎？

Content: Schafer: Don't tell him about gamergate.
Content_zh: Schafer：別跟他說 GamerGate 的事。

Content: No, he knows, he knows.
Content_zh: 不，他知道，他知道。

Content: That's--that's the exciting new challenge for him, will be, um, rebuilding all the things.
Content_zh: 那就是他令人興奮的新挑戰，呃，把所有東西重新建立起來。

Content: Spafford: I knew what was going on.
Content_zh: Spafford：我知道是怎麼回事。

Content: I mean, certainly, lots of people were not happy about the game being split.
Content_zh: 我是說，當然，很多人對遊戲被拆成兩部分不太高興。

Content: Um...so I feel like, yeah, that--that might've been the start of some troubles.
Content_zh: 嗯……所以我覺得，對，那可能就是一些麻煩的開端。

Content: But probably the start of any community troubles was the day that the Kickstarter was made
Content_zh: 但社群問題真正的起點，可能是 Kickstarter 開始的那一天。

Content: and, um, $3.5 million was raised and a lot of people's money
Content_zh: 募集了350萬美金 很多人的錢

Content: was riding on a thing that they had in their head, a vision that they had that this thing was gonna be.
Content_zh: 都押在他們腦中想像的東西上，押在他們對這東西的願景上。

Content: And, you know, it wasn't the way some people wanted it to be.
Content_zh: 而且，你知道，它並不是某些人想要的樣子。

Content: I never want to give up on people who are like, "You know, I'll try everything."
Content_zh: 我永遠不想放棄那些會說：「好，我什麼都願意試試看。」的人。

Content: If people are really mad, a lot of the time you can--
Content_zh: 如果人們真的生氣了，很多時候你可以……

Content: you can--you know, you can bring them around.
Content_zh: 你可以……你知道，你可以把他們拉回來。

Content: There's always some way that you can make them happy again.
Content_zh: 總會有辦法讓他們重新開心起來。

Content: Um...yeah, it's just finding out what that is.
Content_zh: 嗯……對，就是要找出那個方法。

Content: Schafer: It's--it's good timing, 'cause we're--as we're wrapping up the game, we're coming out of production
Content_zh: Schafer：這時機很好，因為我們正在把遊戲收尾，從製作階段走出來。

Content: and getting back into all the community-facing stuff, like getting out the last of the backer rewards.
Content_zh: 重新回到所有面向社群的事情，像是把最後的贊助者回饋送出去。

Content: Like, we still have a lot of these little mini portraits that Bagel has to paint.
Content_zh: 像是 Bagel 還有很多這種迷你小肖像要畫。

Content: So, like, it's come to that point where we're kind of, um,
Content_zh: 所以，事情已經到了這個階段，我們有點，呃……

Content: closin' the loop on the story, big story, and so we'll be facin' the backers a lot,
Content_zh: 要把整個大故事收尾，所以接下來會常常面對贊助者。

Content: and it's good to have a community manager for that.
Content_zh: 有個社群經理來處理這些事是好事。

Content: Okay, so we're just--are we just talking about the launch plans for the game as far as PR and marketing?
Content_zh: 好，所以我們只是……我們是在談遊戲發行時的公關和行銷計畫嗎？

Content: What's--what's your marketing budget on the game?
Content_zh: 這款遊戲的行銷預算是多少？

Content: Rice: Zero...?
Content_zh: Rice：零……？

Content: Schafer: Okay.
Content_zh: Schafer：好吧

Content: Spafford: One of the big things I wanna talk about is how we talk about "Act 2"
Content_zh: Spafford：我想談的一個大重點，是我們要怎麼談「第二幕」。

Content: and how we properly do this, like how we bring this thing out.
Content_zh: 以及要怎麼好好處理這件事，也就是怎麼把它推出去。

Content: Schafer: How do we talk about "Act 2"? Spafford: The biggest thing, right.
Content_zh: Schafer：我們要怎麼談「第二幕」？Spafford：對，這是最大的重點。

Content: Schafer: So just, like, the--what's it called?
Content_zh: Schafer：所以只是，像是……它叫什麼？

Content: Spafford: Well, like, no, as in...
Content_zh: Spafford：嗯，像，不，像……

Content: how are you getting people to think about it?
Content_zh: 你要怎麼讓大家看待它？

Content: You want people to think about it as a whole game, -or as episode two? Rice: Yeah.
Content_zh: 你希望大家把它當成一款完整遊戲，還是第二集？Rice：對。

Content: Schafer: Like, now that it's done, I would like it to just be just "Broken Age,"
Content_zh: Schafer：現在既然完成了，我希望它就只是《Broken Age》。

Content: as if we never split it at all.
Content_zh: 好像我們從來沒有分過一樣

Content: Rice: So, yeah, I think that the hope was, like, a year from now, the story would be more about just the game as it is,
Content_zh: Rice：所以，對，我想我們原本希望，像是一年後，大家談的會更像是這款遊戲本身。

Content: not that it was split or anything.
Content_zh: 不是說它被拆散了

Content: Spafford: When it was released, we did-- people did reviews of it and stuff, right?
Content_zh: Spafford：當它發行時，我們做了…… 人們都做了評論，對吧？

Content: Like, treated it like it was its own thing.
Content_zh: 就像把它當成是它自己的東西一樣

Content: So I wonder how persuadable people will be to just forget that and move on.
Content_zh: 所以我想知道，要說服大家忘掉那件事、繼續往前看會有多容易。

Content: Rice: But I still think we probably have to speak to the people who did already buy it, and--
Content_zh: Rice：但我還是覺得我們可能得跟那些已經買了它的人談談……

Content: Schafer: Yeah, because this is a worry that I do have, that people are like, "When's 'Act 2' coming out?"
Content_zh: Schafer：對，因為我擔心 人們會說，「第二幕」什麼時候出來？

Content: And that they don't see that announcement, -"'Act 2' is out"-- Rice: Yeah.
Content_zh: 他們沒有看到那個公告——「第二幕推出了」——Rice：對。

Content: Schafer: Like, the answer to that question, they might miss the fact that it came out.
Content_zh: Schafer：也就是說，他們可能會錯過這個問題的答案，沒發現它已經出了。

Content: Rice: Yeah.
Content_zh: Rice：是啊。

Content: Yeah, so I think we still need to be saying that, like, for people who purchased "Act 1,"
Content_zh: 對，所以我覺得我們還是需要說，像是，對那些買了「第一幕」的人來說，

Content: they're getting the full game now, updated with "Act 2" content.
Content_zh: 他們現在會拿到完整遊戲，並更新了「第二幕」的內容。

Content: Spafford: Yep. Rice: But...
Content_zh: Spafford：是的。 但是……

Content: Spafford: The PS4 and iOS is definitely a really good chance to just talk about the whole game.
Content_zh: Spafford：PS4 和 iOS 絕對是個很好的機會，可以直接談整款遊戲。

Content: So we've been trying to get people to play the PS4 version,
Content_zh: 所以我們一直試著讓大家玩 PS4 版。

Content: which is a new platform, um, and doesn't have that distinction.
Content_zh: 那是一個新平台，嗯，沒有那種區分。

Content: Like, it's just one game on that platform.
Content_zh: 它在那個平台上就只是一款遊戲。

Content: So the more people we can get to look at that and talk about that,
Content_zh: 所以能讓越多人去看、去談它，

Content: the more-- the more hopeful we are that that can be a thing.
Content_zh: 我們就越有希望讓這件事成真。

Content: Rice: Not--not speaking to just, like, what's gonna happen in "Act 2," you know,
Content_zh: Rice：不是只在講，像是「第二幕」會發生什麼，你知道，

Content: 'cause that's how we've all been thinking about it for a long time, but speaking of, like,
Content_zh: 因為我們都這麼想很久了 但說起來

Content: what is this game?
Content_zh: 這是什麼遊戲？

Content: Spafford: Looking at the full-- the full picture now it's done.
Content_zh: Spafford：現在它完成了，就看整體……完整的樣貌。

Content: Other than that, we're just trying to, you know, beg, borrow, and steal every bit of coverage we can.
Content_zh: 除此之外，我們就是想盡辦法，能爭取多少報導就爭取多少。

Content: Rice: We were able to get a European press tour on zero dollars.
Content_zh: Rice：我們用零預算安排到了一趟歐洲媒體巡迴。

Content: Schafer: I...w-well... Rice: So that's good.
Content_zh: Schafer：我……呃……Rice：所以那很好。

Content: Schafer: We're not paying anything for that?
Content_zh: Schafer：我們不付錢嗎？

Content: Rice: Um, we are paying for three days of a B&B, but they're paying for all of our flights
Content_zh: Rice：嗯，我們要付三天 B&B 的錢，但他們會付我們所有機票。

Content: and our B&B while we're there, so...
Content_zh: 還有我們在那邊住的 B&B，所以……

Content: Um, I think it's all pretty set now.
Content_zh: 嗯，我想現在都安排好了。

Content: So, um, flights and hotels and everything is in there.
Content_zh: 所以，嗯，機票、飯店什麼的都排好了。

Content: There'll be-- basically we get to UK first for the first weekend,
Content_zh: 基本上我們第一個週末先去英國

Content: and then the Monday after that we're doing, like, a bunch of press at Media Molecule.
Content_zh: 然後接下來的星期一，我們會在 Media Molecule 做一堆媒體訪問。

Content: Um, and we'll have Eurogamer, Edge, and The Guardian, uh, all that day in their offices.
Content_zh: 嗯，Eurogamer、Edge 和 The Guardian 那天都會去他們辦公室。

Content: And then, yeah, we have the talk in Vyborg with Peter and Ray.
Content_zh: 然後，對，我們會和 Peter、Ray 在維堡有場座談。

Content: [Music] Spafford: We should pick a day for having press around as well,
Content_zh: [音樂] Spafford：我們也應該選一天讓媒體過來。

Content: and then we can start organizing them.
Content_zh: 然後我們就可以開始組織他們了

Content: [Music] Schafer: So what do you think so far, Marty?
Content_zh: [音樂] Schafer：Marty，目前覺得怎麼樣？

Content: Sliva: I think it's awesome.
Content_zh: Sliva：我覺得很棒

Content: Schafer: Give us your, um, full review now.
Content_zh: Schafer：現在給我們你的，嗯，完整評測吧。

Content: Sliva: Just verbatim, and then you guys can write it down.
Content_zh: Sliva：就逐字念出來，然後你們可以記下來。

Content: Schafer: We need to know the score before we decide whether -you're leaving the room. Sliva: Yeah, okay.
Content_zh: Schafer：我們得先知道分數，再決定你能不能離開這個房間。Sliva：對，好。

Content: Schafer: That's just a common-- Sliva: That's--that's happened-- That's happened before with other publishers, so--
Content_zh: Schafer：這只是很常見的……Sliva：那……那以前其他發行商真的發生過，所以……

Content: Schafer: Really? They're, like... Rice: Oh, God.
Content_zh: Schafer：真的？他們會像……Rice：天啊。

Content: Sliva: I think it's mostly like, like, "You can do an exclusive, but what's the score,"
Content_zh: Sliva：我覺得大多是像：「你可以做獨家，但分數是多少？」

Content: you know what I mean? It's like, well, I'm not gonna do that, then.
Content_zh: 你懂我的意思嗎？就會覺得，好吧，那我就不做了。

Content: Uh, everyone always says, like, "Oh, this is gonna be the adventure game
Content_zh: 呃，大家總是會說：「喔，這會是那款冒險遊戲，

Content: that gets people to play adventure games who haven't played adventure games."
Content_zh: 讓沒玩過冒險遊戲的人也來玩冒險遊戲。」

Content: Like, you--is that bullshit to you?
Content_zh: 像是，你……你覺得那是胡扯嗎？

Content: Like, is that-- is that necessary?
Content_zh: 有必要嗎？

Content: Do you think adventure games need that, -or do you think it's-- Schafer: I've always been--
Content_zh: 你覺得冒險遊戲需要這個嗎 還是你覺得…… 我一直……

Content: I've always been interested in it, because it always seemed like
Content_zh: 我一直對它很感興趣 因為它似乎總是

Content: with adventure games that we were trying to get beyond the-- the limited audience
Content_zh: 在冒險遊戲這件事上，我們一直試著突破那個……有限的受眾。

Content: of people currently playing--playing games.
Content_zh: 也就是目前有在玩……玩遊戲的人。

Content: Not that they're limited, but there-- there was a, back in the '90s,
Content_zh: 不是說他們有限，而是……在 90 年代時，

Content: a small group of the culture at large that was playing games at all.
Content_zh: 整體文化裡真的有在玩遊戲的人只是很小一群。

Content: And it seemed like there are all these people who weren't playing games and they're like, hey,
Content_zh: 而且感覺外面有很多沒在玩遊戲的人，我們會想，嘿，

Content: I think you would like a game that, well, it's about story, where you don't have to worry about getting shot in the head,
Content_zh: 我覺得你會喜歡一款重視故事、又不用擔心被爆頭的遊戲。

Content: and, like, you could play at your own pace and it involved themes that weren't
Content_zh: 而且你可以照自己的步調玩，裡面有一些遊戲當時沒在處理的主題。

Content: being covered in games, you know.
Content_zh: 你知道。

Content: So we always, you know, we always, you know...
Content_zh: 我們總是……

Content: People would tell us, like, "This is the only game that my girlfriend will play with me."
Content_zh: 有人會跟我們說：「這是我女朋友唯一會跟我一起玩的遊戲。」

Content: And it was--that was always the story.
Content_zh: 一直如此

Content: Like, there's all these people out there who, you know, why aren't they playing games?
Content_zh: 像是，外面有那麼多人，你知道，他們為什麼不玩遊戲？

Content: And I think this is the game they would like.
Content_zh: 我覺得這就是他們會喜歡的遊戲。

Content: But, um, you know, it's always been hard to reach those people, and, um, and make them feel like they would like--
Content_zh: 但，嗯，你知道，要接觸到那些人一直都很難，嗯，要讓他們覺得自己會喜歡……

Content: You know, they think of themselves as someone who would not play video games.
Content_zh: 你知道，他們認為自己就是不會玩電玩的人。

Content: It's hard to get past that identity problem, almost.
Content_zh: 要跨過那種身分認同的問題真的很難。

Content: Sliva: Totally. Schafer: So I do think about it, but I don't think it's necessary.
Content_zh: Sliva：完全同意。Schafer：所以我確實會想這件事，但我不覺得那是必要的。

Content: Like, I think it's good enough to make a game that people--
Content_zh: 我想做個遊戲就足夠了

Content: and that's what this Kickstarter is all about.
Content_zh: 而這就是這次 Kickstarter 的重點。

Content: Like, there are these people who said, "We like adventure games just the way they are."
Content_zh: 像是，有些人說：「我們就是喜歡冒險遊戲原本的樣子。」

Content: And they don't need to completely revolutionize with--with how they interact.
Content_zh: 它們不需要在互動方式上徹底革命。

Content: They don't need to be a different genre.
Content_zh: 它們不需要變成不同類型。

Content: They just, "We like the old adventure games and we wanna play another one."
Content_zh: 他們只是說：「我們喜歡老派冒險遊戲，我們想再玩一款。」

Content: And knowing they were out there and we were making the game for them, uh,
Content_zh: 知道他們在外面 我們為他們做遊戲

Content: is what made it special this time around.
Content_zh: 這次才讓它變得特別

Content: Rice: There's always been, like, conversation coming up here and there about how we're gonna give backers the codes,
Content_zh: Rice：一直都有些零星的討論，關於我們要怎麼把序號給贊助者。

Content: or if we're gonna give them early access to the game or anything like that.
Content_zh: 或者要不要讓他們提前玩到遊戲之類的。

Content: And I feel like it's never been, like, fully talked about and decided upon.
Content_zh: 我覺得這件事從來沒有被完整討論、做出決定。

Content: Schafer: I was--I wanted to give it just the--the day before, -the 24 hours before. Rice: Yeah.
Content_zh: Schafer：我本來……我想就給他們前一天，提前 24 小時。Rice：對。

Content: Spafford: Yeah, if it's possible without too much mess, then I think that people will really appreciate it.
Content_zh: Spafford：對，如果不會太麻煩、做得到的話，我覺得大家會很感激。

Content: And in fact, we've set the embargo for press for the same time
Content_zh: 事實上，我們把媒體禁評解除時間設在同一個時間。

Content: as the backers getting their keys so that, you know, everyone's getting
Content_zh: 也就是贊助者拿到序號的時候，這樣，你知道，大家都會拿到。

Content: their thing at the same time.
Content_zh: 同時拿到自己的東西。

Content: Offscreen: Yeah, 'cause the embargo thing was such an issue last time.
Content_zh: 畫外音：對，因為上次禁評那件事真的鬧得很大。

Content: Spafford: I think there is all kinds of emotions about that.
Content_zh: Spafford：我覺得大家對那件事有各種情緒。

Content: Rice: Tim, did you hear that? Schafer: What?
Content_zh: Rice：Tim，你聽到了嗎？Schafer：什麼？

Content: Rice: "Digital Trends" posted an article, "Why We're Breaking the 'Broken Age' Embargo."
Content_zh: Rice：「Digital Trends」發了一篇文章，標題是「我們為什麼要打破《Broken Age》的禁評」。

Content: Schafer: Tell 'em maybe he didn't agree to anything, but he knows he's bein' a shit.
Content_zh: Schafer：告訴他們，也許他沒有同意任何事，但他知道自己這樣很爛。

Content: Bailey: I was looking over all of our news broadcasts, and basically all the exposure went out
Content_zh: Bailey：我看了一下我們所有的新聞報導，基本上所有曝光都已經出去了。

Content: when the backers got it, like, two weeks ago.
Content_zh: 就是在贊助者兩週前拿到遊戲的時候。

Content: Spafford: I think that whole situation probably should have been done a bit differently.
Content_zh: Spafford：我覺得整件事可能應該用稍微不同的方式處理。

Content: I think we learned a lot from that, by the sounds of things, or at least I hope so.
Content_zh: 聽起來我們從中學到了很多，至少我希望如此。

Content: And I've certainly watched that episode and--and thought, "Okay, we need to not do that."
Content_zh: 我也確實看過那一集，然後想：「好，我們不能那樣做。」

Content: Yeah, so, it--today is Friday.
Content_zh: 對，所以……今天是星期五。

Content: Uh, on Sunday night, we're gonna send out backer keys to people, which is pretty exciting.
Content_zh: 呃，星期天晚上，我們要把贊助者序號寄出去給大家，這還滿令人興奮的。

Content: Um, 24 hours later, the game will unlock on Steam and be pushed out, uh, around the world
Content_zh: 嗯，24 小時後，遊戲會在 Steam 解鎖，並且在全世界推出。

Content: onto however many millions of platforms that we're doing.
Content_zh: 推到我們正在做的那幾百萬個平台上。

Content: Um, so by Tuesday, uh, morning when we all come into the office,
Content_zh: 嗯，所以到了星期二早上，大家進辦公室的時候，

Content: the game will be out, everyone will be playing, no one will have any problems, everything will be fine,
Content_zh: 遊戲就已經推出了，大家都在玩，沒有人會遇到任何問題，一切都會很順利。

Content: and everyone will be having a good time.
Content_zh: 大家都會很開心。

Content: That's--that's the gist of it.
Content_zh: 大概就是這樣。

Content: Hansen: Flawless. Franzke: What could go wrong, right?
Content_zh: Hansen：完美無瑕。Franzke：會出什麼差錯呢，對吧？

Content: Hansen: Yeah, don't say that.
Content_zh: Hansen：對，別這樣說。

Content: Rice: It's all gonna be perfect.
Content_zh: Rice：一切都會很完美。

Content: Hansen: It--it may not be perfect.
Content_zh: Hansen：它……它可能不會完美。

Content: Rice: Um, all right.
Content_zh: Rice：嗯，好吧。

Content: All right.
Content_zh: 好吧

Content: Let's put out the Kickstarter update?
Content_zh: 我們來發 Kickstarter 更新嗎？

Content: Spafford: Let's do it.
Content_zh: Spafford：來吧。

Content: Schafer: Yo, yo, yo, yo, yo.
Content_zh: Schafer：Yo、yo、yo、yo、yo。

Content: Yo, yo, yo, yo, yo.
Content_zh: Yo、yo、yo、yo、yo。

Content: Hi, everybody.
Content_zh: 大家好。

Content: Hello, hello, thank you for waiting.
Content_zh: 哈囉、哈囉，謝謝大家等待。

Content: Thanks for-- sorry about the delay.
Content_zh: 謝謝……抱歉耽誤大家。

Content: It's still so interesting, Melina just magically appears there.
Content_zh: 還是好有趣，Melina 就這樣神奇地出現在那裡。

Content: Hello, Melina, nice to see you.
Content_zh: 你好，Melina，很高興見到你。

Content: Awesome, awesome.
Content_zh: 太棒了，太棒了。

Content: Okay. Hi, everybody.
Content_zh: 好，大家好。

Content: Uh, today I've been very preoccupied 'cause "Broken Age" is in the hands of our backers
Content_zh: 呃，今天我一直很分心，因為《Broken Age》已經到了我們贊助者手上。

Content: and people are playing it, which is amazing!
Content_zh: 而且大家都在玩，這太棒了！

Content: Yeah!
Content_zh: 耶！

Content: Offscreen: Do you wanna look at the forum right now?
Content_zh: 畫外音：你想看看論壇嗎？

Content: Schafer: Okay.
Content_zh: Schafer：好吧

Content: Let's go to the forum, shall we?
Content_zh: 我們去論壇吧

Content: "Spoiler: Need hint urgently." Ah! Ah!
Content_zh: 「劇透：急需提示。」啊！啊！

Content: Take that, right?
Content_zh: 接招吧，對吧？

Content: Right?
Content_zh: 對吧？

Content: Where's the general...
Content_zh: 一般討論區在哪……

Content: "Let's talk about how much we love the game" thread?
Content_zh: 「來聊聊我們有多愛這款遊戲」的討論串？

Content: Let's see.
Content_zh: 我看看

Content: Discussion and feedback.
Content_zh: 討論與意見回饋。

Content: "I finished this morning.
Content_zh: 「我今天早上破關了。

Content: What an incredible game!" That's all I'm gonna read.
Content_zh: 多麼不可思議的遊戲！」我就只唸這段。

Content: That's it.
Content_zh: 好了

Content: "Everything about 'Act 2' has improved the game dramatically.
Content_zh: 「『第二幕』的每個部分都大幅改善了遊戲。

Content: The puzzles are a lot more detailed.
Content_zh: 謎題細節多了很多。

Content: Required me to pull out a notepad." Yay.
Content_zh: 讓我不得不拿出筆記本。」耶。

Content: That's nice. I'm glad it makes-- I get excited when I pull out a notepad.
Content_zh: 真好。我很高興它讓……我拿出筆記本的時候就會很興奮。

Content: "I liked 'Act 1,' but it was a little linear.
Content_zh: 「我喜歡『第一幕』，但它有點線性。

Content: 'Act 2' is perfect.
Content_zh: 『第二幕』很完美。

Content: And to Tim and everyone at Double Fine, I really appreciate you and what you've done so much."
Content_zh: 還有給 Tim 和 Double Fine 的所有人，我真的非常感謝你們和你們所做的一切。」

Content: Well, that's nice.
Content_zh: 很好

Content: Ah! The game's out.
Content_zh: 啊！遊戲推出了。

Content: I mean, it really comes out tomorrow, or midnight tonight.
Content_zh: 我是說，它其實明天才正式推出，或是今晚午夜。

Content: But it's out, the review embargo is lifted, we got a 9.2 from "Game Informer,"
Content_zh: 但它已經出來了，評測解禁限制也解除了，我們在 Game Informer 拿到 9.2 分。

Content: and it should be good for launch, official launch day tomorrow,
Content_zh: 明天正式發行日應該會很順利。

Content: where it'll be out on all platforms and everywhere.
Content_zh: 到時候會在所有平台、所有地方推出。

Content: Um, so now we just sit around and read reviews and pretend we don't care about 'em.
Content_zh: 嗯，所以現在我們就坐著看評測，假裝我們不在乎。

Content: That's the...regular thing.
Content_zh: 那是…… 平常的事

Content: I'm gonna sit there, like, all night just waiting for them to come in.
Content_zh: 我大概會整晚坐在那裡，等著評測一篇篇出來。

Content: You see one, you're like, "Oh, that-- that was awesome! I'm gonna read one more!
Content_zh: 你看到一篇，就會想：「喔，那篇……那篇太棒了！我要再讀一篇！」

Content: Oh, that was bad, I'm not gonna-- not gonna read any m--
Content_zh: 「喔，那篇很糟，我不……我不要再讀任……」

Content: Oh, that one was nice!" And then it all evens out.
Content_zh: 「喔，那篇不錯！」然後一切就平均掉了。

Content: I just was reading, like, two different reviews, one talking about being too hard
Content_zh: 我剛剛在讀兩篇不同的評測，一篇說太難。

Content: and one being, it's too easy.
Content_zh: 另一篇說太簡單。

Content: And, uh, you know, that just seems to be the way with those games.
Content_zh: 呃，你知道，這種遊戲好像就是這樣。

Content: They're just, like, um...
Content_zh: 他們只是……

Content: people--people react strongly and very subjectively and, um...I don't know.
Content_zh: 大家……大家的反應很強烈，也非常主觀，嗯……我不知道。

Content: Like, I feel like it takes years, almost, for people to really decide
Content_zh: 我覺得要花好幾年 人們才能真正決定

Content: how they feel about these games.
Content_zh: 他們對這些遊戲到底是什麼感覺。

Content: So 10 years from now we'll find out if it was any good.
Content_zh: 所以十年後我們就會知道它到底好不好了。

Content: Offscreen: It seems a bit strange that there's review scores for "Act 1," "Act 2--"
Content_zh: 畫外音：感覺有點奇怪，因為「第一幕」、「第二幕——」都有評分。

Content: And the whole thing.
Content_zh: 還有整款遊戲。

Content: Schafer: Yeah, it's a mess... it's a mess.
Content_zh: Schafer：是啊，一團糟…… 一團糟。

Content: Never split your game in half.
Content_zh: 永遠不要把你的遊戲拆成兩半。

Content: Rice: By the way, there's a lot of people talking about it, so that's good.
Content_zh: Rice：順帶一提，現在有很多人在討論它，所以這是好事。

Content: Um, and tomorrow we're partying, and I think I sent an invite out to everybody.
Content_zh: 明天我們要開派對 我想我給大家發了邀請函

Content: So, bar crawl, I think we're just starting at Victory Hall, 'cause it's close and we can walk there,
Content_zh: 所以，跑酒吧的話，我想我們就從 Victory Hall 開始，因為那裡很近，可以走過去。

Content: and then we're gonna go-- Schafer: And then the hardcore. Rice: Chinatown.
Content_zh: 然後我們會去——Schafer：然後重頭戲。Rice：唐人街。

Content: Schafer: To people who care about history and wanna relive history through the bars of Chinatown and beyond.
Content_zh: Schafer：敬那些在乎歷史、想透過唐人街和其他地方的酒吧重溫歷史的人。

Content: Rice: So, tonight at 9:00 our time, midnight Eastern, is when all the other platforms will go up.
Content_zh: Rice：所以今晚我們時間 9 點，也就是東岸午夜，其他所有平台都會上架。

Content: Bottle Opener: In the name of Lars Halford, open up!
Content_zh: Bottle Opener：以拉斯·哈爾福德之名，開門！

Content: Franzke: Okay, so we've got Ouya lined up.
Content_zh: Franzke：好，所以 Ouya 已經準備好了。

Content: I guess we just need to press that button.
Content_zh: 我想我們只要按那個按鈕就好。

Content: Rice: The button?
Content_zh: Rice：按鈕？

Content: Uh, Spaff figured out some way to wire this weird Divekick button...
Content_zh: 呃，Spaff 想出辦法，把這個奇怪的 Divekick 按鈕接上……

Content: Spafford: It's not that weird! Rice: ...to launching the game.
Content_zh: Spafford：才沒那麼奇怪！Rice：……用來發布遊戲。

Content: -Cheers. Franzke: Well, cheers to you guys.
Content_zh: －乾杯。Franzke：好，敬你們。

Content: Spafford: It's okay, we're toasting the five-- four-minute countdown
Content_zh: Spafford：沒關係，我們是在為五……四分鐘倒數乾杯，

Content: to pressing the blue button.
Content_zh: 倒數到按下藍色按鈕。

Content: Do you want the blue pill, or the yellow pill?
Content_zh: 你要藍色藥丸，還是黃色藥丸？

Content: Rice: Oh my God, everything explodes.
Content_zh: Rice：天啊，一切都爆炸了。

Content: Franzke: The game gets deleted, obliterated from the server, so don't press the yellow button.
Content_zh: Franzke：遊戲會被刪掉，從伺服器上徹底消失，所以不要按黃色按鈕。

Content: Spafford: All the money is refunded.
Content_zh: Spafford：所有錢都退回去了。

Content: Franzke: The code gets unwritten, the art unmade.
Content_zh: Franzke：程式碼被取消編寫，美術也沒做過。

Content: Rice: Yep.
Content_zh: Rice：是的。

Content: Spafford: What are we gonna do for four minutes, you guys?
Content_zh: Spafford：各位，我們這四分鐘要幹嘛？

Content: We could read Twitter for a bit.
Content_zh: 可以看一下 Twitter。

Content: Spafford: People are really happy. Rice: The reviews have been -really good. Spafford: Yep.
Content_zh: Spafford：大家都很開心。Rice：評價真的很好。Spafford：對。

Content: Rice: Just, like, the way they describe the puzzles and the writing,
Content_zh: Rice：就像他們描述謎題跟劇本的方式，

Content: it makes it-- makes it feel like people actually really got it,
Content_zh: 讓人覺得……覺得大家真的懂我們想做什麼，

Content: -which is nice. Spafford: We should get ready, because we're about to--to go into...
Content_zh: －這很棒。Spafford：我們該準備了，因為我們就要……要進入……

Content: Rice: Let's do it, you guys.
Content_zh: Rice：來吧，各位。

Content: Spafford: You should. Franzke: Okay, Greg, It's hovering over the button.
Content_zh: Spafford：你該按。Franzke：好，Greg，游標停在按鈕上了。

Content: Rice: You do this one. Franzke: I do this one?
Content_zh: Rice：這個你來。Franzke：這個我來？

Content: This is Steam; this is the big one.
Content_zh: 這是 Steam，這個最重要。

Content: Rice: I know. You do this one. Franzke: All right.
Content_zh: Rice：我知道。這個你來。Franzke：好。

Content: Spafford: Press the "Okay" button!
Content_zh: Spafford：按「OK」按鈕！

Content: Franzke: All right, here it goes!
Content_zh: Franzke：好，要來了！

Content: It's live on Steam.
Content_zh: Steam 上架了。

Content: Rice: Yay. Spafford: Oh, my God.
Content_zh: Rice：耶。Spafford：天啊。

Content: Rice: Okay. Spafford: All right, next one.
Content_zh: Rice：好。Spafford：好，下一個。

Content: Franzke: Okay, here's iOS.
Content_zh: Franzke：好，這是 iOS。

Content: Spafford: Oh my God, oh my God, oh my God.
Content_zh: Spafford：天啊，天啊，天啊。

Content: Rice: Oh, man...
Content_zh: Rice：天啊……

Content: Franzke: All right, here, Spaff.
Content_zh: Franzke：好，來，Spaff。

Content: Franzke: Spaff, you do the OS X App Store.
Content_zh: Franzke：Spaff，你來弄 OS X App Store。

Content: Spafford: Release this version.
Content_zh: Spafford：發布這個版本。

Content: Franzke: Release!
Content_zh: Franzke：發布！

Content: I'll do Ouya.
Content_zh: Ouya 我來。

Content: Rice: Yep, there you go.
Content_zh: Rice：對，就是這樣。

Content: You're in that one.
Content_zh: 你已經進去了。

Content: Spafford: Ouya's live?
Content_zh: Spafford：Ouya 上架了？

Content: Franzke: Waiting--yep. Spafford: Okay.
Content_zh: Franzke：等一下……對。Spafford：好。

Content: Franzke: Just Google Play.
Content_zh: Franzke：只剩 Google Play。

Content: Rice: Okay, wait, is this the last one?
Content_zh: Rice：等等，這是最後一個嗎？

Content: Franzke: Yeah. Rice: Let's all do this one.
Content_zh: Franzke：對。Rice：這個大家一起來。

Content: Franzke: Okay. Ready?
Content_zh: Franzke：好吧 好了嗎？

Content: Rice: Final build, one, two, three, go.
Content_zh: Rice：最終版本，一、二、三，按。

Content: [Music] Franzke: But, yes, this is my last, I guess,
Content_zh: [音樂] Franzke：不過，對，這應該是我最後一個，

Content: full day in the office, one could say.
Content_zh: 可以說是在辦公室待一整天的日子。

Content: Not that I'm actually gonna do any work, but, um...
Content_zh: 倒不是我真的會做什麼工作，不過，嗯……

Content: Today is "Broken Age" launch day, officially.
Content_zh: 今天是《Broken Age》正式發行日。

Content: Uh, so lots of parties lined up.
Content_zh: 呃，所以排了很多派對。

Content: I am just gonna pretend they're all for me, for my leave--for one of my leaving parties.
Content_zh: 我就假裝那些都是為我辦的，為了我離職……為了其中一場離職派對。

Content: Uh...but yeah, it's--it's weird.
Content_zh: 呃……不過對，這……這很怪。

Content: It's weird. What is that? Oh.
Content_zh: 很怪。那是什麼？喔。

Content: Oh, because we don't actually need this anymore?
Content_zh: 喔，因為我們其實不需要這個了？

Content: That's true, I guess I don't need to talk while-- Brr!
Content_zh: 也對，我想我不用一邊……喔！

Content: There's that.
Content_zh: 就這樣。

Content: All right?
Content_zh: 好嗎？

Content: Rice: We just put out the new trailer, so we're trying to, like, spread those around, um...
Content_zh: Rice：我們剛發布了新預告，所以正在想辦法把它散播出去，嗯……

Content: Offscreen: Elijah Wood? Rice: I did see that.
Content_zh: 畫外音：Elijah Wood？Rice：我有看到。

Content: So Elijah just tweeted, like, thirty minutes ago about the game.
Content_zh: 所以 Elijah 大概 30 分鐘前才剛發推文講這款遊戲。

Content: Right there, see? Boom.
Content_zh: 就在那裡，看到了嗎？砰。

Content: Yay, Elijah.
Content_zh: 耶，Elijah。

Content: Like you see right now, we're at, like, twenty-five or so on the list.
Content_zh: 像你們現在看到的，我們大概在榜上第 25 名左右。

Content: Um...and then the hope is that that'll start going up, so, by a couple things.
Content_zh: 嗯……然後希望它會開始往上爬，靠幾件事推一把。

Content: One is, we can check and see now if it's getting featured.
Content_zh: 第一，我們現在可以看看它有沒有被放到精選。

Content: Okay, so there it is.
Content_zh: 好，在那裡。

Content: Yay. So this showed up.
Content_zh: 耶。所以它出現了。

Content: So this'll help, especially since it's on sale.
Content_zh: 這會有幫助，尤其是它正在特價。

Content: Um, that's always nice.
Content_zh: 嗯，這總是好事。

Content: On Steam people see that sale number.
Content_zh: 在 Steam 上，大家會看到那個特價標示。

Content: We do get daily Android and iOS sales.
Content_zh: 我們每天都會拿到 Android 和 iOS 的銷售數字。

Content: PS4 probably won't, uh, give us any kinda inclination for, like, a week or so, maybe, um, maybe more.
Content_zh: PS4 大概一週左右都不會給我們什麼方向，也許更久。

Content: Spafford: They're the most interesting ones, I think.
Content_zh: Spafford：我想他們是最有趣的

Content: Rice: Yeah, that'll be interesting, 'cause it's new.
Content_zh: Rice：是啊，這會很有趣，因為它是新的。

Content: Um...brand-new sales and it's reviewing well there and everything.
Content_zh: 嗯……全新的銷售數字，而且那邊評價也很好之類的。

Content: So, uh, I think we'll have a better idea in, like, a couple weeks across all platforms,

Content: on that first day.

Content: It made you feel like the world is a much better place than you thought it was.

Content_zh: 除非我站起來引起大家的注意 不然一切似乎都不會發生

Content_zh: 然後向所有人說聲謝謝

Content: McConnell: Well, thank you.
Content_zh: McConnell：謝謝你

Content: When you've worked on something for--for a good long time,
Content_zh: 當你在某件事上投入了……投入了很長一段時間，

Content: you know, over two years, you know, two and a half for me, three I think for the--
Content_zh: 你知道，超過兩年，對我來說是兩年半，對整個……

Content: for--for the--the team at large, um, uh, it's hard not to be, you know,
Content_zh: 對……對整個團隊來說，我想是三年，嗯，呃，真的很難不……

Content: a little misty-eyed about it.
Content_zh: 有點眼眶泛淚。

Content: It's-it's like getting to the end of the game, when you play the game, um,
Content_zh: 這……這就像你玩遊戲玩到結尾時，嗯，

Content: where--where you have to, um, say, oh, well, you know, these characters
Content_zh: 到那個……到那個你必須，嗯，說，喔，好吧，你知道，這些角色

Content: have become my friends and--and now I have to say, you know, goodbye to the story
Content_zh: 已經變成我的朋友，而……而現在我得跟這個故事說再見，

Content: and goodbye to the characters.
Content_zh: 也跟這些角色說再見。

Content: And so there's a certain amount of sadness, I guess.
Content_zh: 所以我想多少會有一點難過。

Content: And--and, uh, certainly, um, you know, there's a little relief.
Content_zh: 而……而且，呃，當然，嗯，也會有一點鬆了一口氣。

Content: It's always nice to be-- actually to say, oh, finally, you know?
Content_zh: 能說「喔，終於」總是件好事，你懂吧？

Content: Um, you know, but there's also that, oh, well, you know...
Content_zh: 嗯，你知道，不過也還是會有那種，喔，好吧，你知道……

Content: I mean, that's...
Content_zh: 我的意思是，那是……

Content: that's like, uh...um...
Content_zh: 就像…… 嗯……

Content: It's, uh, an--an era, you know?
Content_zh: 這是，呃，一個……一個時代，你懂嗎？

Content: A really big project like that, you love like--like a-- like a child.
Content_zh: 像那樣的大型專案，你會像愛……像愛孩子一樣愛它。

Content: And, uh, letting it go out into the world is a--is a-- is an emotional moment.
Content_zh: 而，呃，讓它走進世界，是……是……是很感性的時刻。

Content_zh: Schafer：需要提示就打給我

Content: All right.

Content: Now, then...
Content_zh: 那麼……

Content: [Laughter] That's what you get for being cute!
Content_zh: [笑聲] 這就是你裝可愛的下場！

Content: TV: Oliver, safe travels and best of luck for the future.
Content_zh: 電視：Oliver，一路順風，祝你未來一切順利。

Content: And everybody else, stay awesome, stay strong, and find joy in the things that you do.
Content_zh: 其他所有人，保持帥氣、保持堅強，並在自己做的事裡找到快樂。

Content: -Bye. Schafer: Bye!
Content_zh: －再見。Schafer：再見！

Content: Kipnis: Aw...
Content_zh: Kipnis：啊……

Content: Wow.
Content_zh: 哇

Content: Schafer: Awww.
Content_zh: Schafer：喔……

Content: TV: "Broken Age" got released...
Content_zh: 電視：《Broken Age》發行了……

Content: Kipnis: Oh!
Content_zh: Kipnis：哦！

Content: Schafer: Majus!
Content_zh: Schafer：馬尤斯！

Content: TV: I'm so happy for all of you.
Content_zh: 電視：我真的替你們所有人高興。

Content: Congratulations.
Content_zh: 恭喜

Content: You finished this work of art, and now everybody can see and play it
Content_zh: 你們完成了這件藝術品，現在所有人都能看到、能玩到它，

Content: and it's wonderful!
Content_zh: 太棒了！

Content: [Laughter] Struggled with problems and obstacles.
Content_zh: [笑聲] 一路經歷了難題和障礙。

Content: Ups and downs are a normal part
Content_zh: 起起伏伏本來就是

Content: of the whole project.
Content_zh: 整個專案的一部分。

Content: And you should embrace that.
Content_zh: 你們應該擁抱這一點。

Content: It's part of it.
Content_zh: 那就是其中一部分。

Content: "Broken Age" is made out of the ups and downs.
Content_zh: 《Broken Age》就是由這些起伏組成的。

Content: I am really emotional right now.
Content_zh: 我現在真的很激動。

Content: I wish I could talk to you for real right now.
Content_zh: 我希望現在能親自跟你們說話。

Content: And hope I see you again soon in the future.
Content_zh: 也希望不久後能再見到你們。

Content: And, uh, let's make more games.
Content_zh: 然後，呃，我們繼續做更多遊戲吧。

Content: Let's show the world how the sausage gets made.
Content_zh: 讓我們向全世界展示遊戲是怎麼做出來的。

Content: And, um, yeah.
Content_zh: 然後，嗯，對。

Content: I love you guys.
Content_zh: 我愛你們

Content: Crowd: Aw!
Content_zh: 群眾：喔！

Content: TV: And...yeah.
Content_zh: 電視：然後……對。

Content: Let's, uh, let's--we--we'll-- we'll talk in person, so let's not do this.
Content_zh: 我們，呃，我們……我們會……我們會當面聊，所以就別在這裡講了。

Content: [Laughter] Schafer: Let's not let this get weird.
Content_zh: [笑聲] Schafer：別讓這件事變尷尬。

Content: TV: Bye. Bye.
Content_zh: 電視：再見。再見。

Content: I love you.
Content_zh: 我愛你。

Content: Crowd: Bye, Majus!
Content_zh: 群眾：再見，Majus！

Content: Schafer: I mean, crowdfunding was so great, and is so great in a lot of ways.
Content_zh: Schafer：我的意思是，群眾募資真的很棒，而且在很多方面都很棒。

Content: Like, it's--the--the, um, the relationship you have with the community
Content_zh: 像是，你跟社群之間的那種關係，

Content: and the-- and the nature of the money is so, um, perfect for what you wanna do,
Content_zh: 還有……還有那筆資金的性質，都非常，嗯，非常適合你想做的事，

Content: do something creative and--and community focused without worrying about ties to people
Content_zh: 做一些有創意、以社群為核心的東西，不用擔心跟某些人有利益牽扯，

Content: who might wanna try to influence your game because they gave you money.
Content_zh: 因為那些人可能會因為給了你錢，就想影響你的遊戲。

Content: Um...it's--it's weird because it just happened to time out--
Content_zh: 嗯……這……這很奇怪，因為時間點剛好……

Content: it happened to coincide with a weird phase of the Internet where everyone's super angry all the time and it just, like--
Content_zh: 這剛好碰上網路上一個很怪的時期，大家總是超憤怒，然後就……

Content: it was hard to be that open and transparent and give everybody--
Content_zh: 要這麼開放透明，讓大家……

Content: It's like we opened up a little tray of, you know, bludgeoning instruments so that--
Content_zh: 感覺就像我們打開了一個小托盤，裡面放滿了可以拿來揍人的工具，然後……

Content: for people could beat us up with.
Content_zh: 讓大家可以拿來揍我們。

Content: And then people picked 'em up and just beat us up with them.
Content_zh: 結果大家就拿起來，真的用它們來揍我們。

Content: And we're like, huh, that was an interesting experiment.
Content_zh: 我們就想，嗯，這實驗還真有意思。

Content: We probably shouldn't have given all those weapons to all the people who are mad.
Content_zh: 我們大概不該把那些武器都交給那些生氣的人。

Content: But, um, if that had not erupted at that moment, you know, maybe I would've thought--
Content_zh: 不過，嗯，如果那件事沒有在那個時候爆發，也許我會想……

Content: 'Cause at the beginning, the transparency was nothing but, like,
Content_zh: 因為一開始，所謂的透明化根本就只是……

Content: um, hugs and, uh, high-fives all around.
Content_zh: 到處都是擁抱和擊掌。

Content: Like, everybody at our-- you know, our PAX meetups, like, that was our community experience,
Content_zh: 像是我們的……你知道，我們的 PAX 聚會，那就是我們當時的社群體驗，

Content: was everyone just telling us how much they were excited about the game
Content_zh: 大家都在跟我們說他們有多期待這款遊戲，

Content: and how much they loved us and everything.
Content_zh: 還有他們多愛我們之類的。

Content: And then when we announced the change in the schedule, just all that-- just people just--
Content_zh: 然後當我們宣布時程變更時，所有那些……大家就……

Content: it seemed like there was a big crowd of people just ready to-- just really super mad.
Content_zh: 感覺有一大群人早就準備好要……真的超憤怒。

Content: And if you Google, like, um, "'Broken Age,' broken promises" on the Internet-- which I do every night--
Content_zh: 如果你在網路上 Google，嗯，「《Broken Age》，broken promises」——我每天晚上都會這麼做——

Content: if you Google that, you'll see all these angry articles.
Content_zh: 如果你去 Google，就會看到一堆憤怒的文章。

Content: Not just from, like, random people on Twitter, but, like, these journalists who are like,
Content_zh: 不只是 Twitter 上的路人，還有那些記者，他們會說：

Content: "They really let everybody down with this and they should really be sorry."
Content_zh: 「他們真的讓大家失望了，他們真的應該道歉。」

Content: And, um...
and here we are at the end and we're shipping the game and it took a long time,
Content_zh: 而現在我們走到最後，要推出遊戲了，這花了很久，

Content: but it's everything we said we were gonna do.
Content_zh: 但這就是我們說過要做的事。

Content: Uh...and I want to mail all those people individually and be like,
Content_zh: 呃……我很想一個一個寄信給那些人，跟他們說：

Content: "It's time for you to apologize about that article." But I don't think they feel like they were wrong.
Content_zh: 「你該為那篇文章道歉了。」但我覺得他們不會覺得自己錯了。

Content: I think they feel like-- that we still really messed up.
Content_zh: 我覺得他們還是覺得……我們真的搞砸了。

Content: But, um, I don't.
Content_zh: 可是，嗯，我不這麼覺得。

Content: I feel like...
Content_zh: 我覺得……

Content: I feel--isn't it great to know I've learned nothing?
Content_zh: 我覺得……知道我什麼都沒學到，不是很棒嗎？

Content: But I feel like...
Content_zh: 但我覺得……

Content: [Laughter] I feel, like, officially that--
Content_zh: [笑聲] 我覺得，正式來說……

Content: I feel like I just need to make an official announcement that I don't apologize for anything,
Content_zh: 我覺得我只需要正式宣布，我不為任何事道歉，

Content: because I feel really good about it.
Content_zh: 因為我對這件事感覺很好。

Content: And if we had shipped one day earlier, the game would've been one day buggier.
Content_zh: 如果我們早一天推出，遊戲就會多一天的 bug。

Content: And, um, if we had shipped the game that the original $3.3 million paid for,
Content_zh: 而且，嗯，如果我們只做出原本 330 萬美元能負擔的遊戲，

Content: it would've been small.
Content_zh: 那它會很小。

Content: And I like the game that we made instead, and we put a lot of our own money into it,
Content_zh: 我喜歡我們後來做出來的遊戲，而且我們也投入了很多自己的錢，

Content: and I think I'm really, really happy.
Content_zh: 我覺得我真的非常、非常開心。

Content: This is the game, this is what the game kinda said that it wanted to be, about this size,
Content_zh: 這就是這款遊戲，這大概就是它自己想成為的樣子，差不多就是這個規模，

Content: and we did a really good version of it.
Content_zh: 而我們做出了一個很棒的版本。

Content: And so I don't apologize for anything.
Content_zh: 所以我不為任何事道歉。

Content: [Music] Raz: He makes me laugh.
Content_zh: [音樂] Raz：他真的很好笑。

Content: "And that's why it's called a game." Schafer: Yay!
Content_zh: 「所以它才叫遊戲。」Schafer：耶！

Content: Uh, even though you like basketball...

Content_zh: Schafer：太棒了 是的

Content: Well, that's awesome.

Content: Schafer: I wish I really knew right now if I could say 100% what I learned from all that.
Content_zh: Schafer：我希望我現在真的能百分之百說清楚，我從這一切學到了什麼。

Content: 'Cause some of it's still, you know, uh, too soon.
Content_zh: 因為有些還太早了

Content_zh: 因為我有一部分會想，我現在就是不想告訴任何人

Content: about what we're working on right now.
Content_zh: 我們正在做什麼。

Content: Like, I feel just kinda, like, so exposed that I just wanna be--
Content_zh: 我覺得自己有點太暴露了，所以只想……

Content: I just wanna have some secrets for a little while, just as a kinda break from that.
Content_zh: 我只想暫時保留一點祕密，當作喘口氣。

Content: I had a pleasant trip down memory lane making an adventure game, but I remembered
Content_zh: 做冒險遊戲讓我愉快地重溫了過去，但我也想起來

Content: how hard they are to make.
Content_zh: 它們有多難做。

Content: And, um, I don't think my next game will be an adventure game.
Content_zh: 而且，嗯，我不覺得我的下一款遊戲會是冒險遊戲。

Content: I wouldn't say I'd never make one again.
Content_zh: 我不會說我再也不做冒險遊戲。

Content: But in the end, I really-- I really think the team put together a beautiful game,
Content_zh: 但到最後，我真的……我真的覺得團隊做出了一款很美的遊戲，

Content: and that's what I'm most happy with.
Content_zh: 這就是我最開心的

Content: 'Kay.
Content_zh: 好

Content_zh: 好，如果這件事結果不對，接下來會很尷尬。

Content: Not that I'm gonna complain, 'cause it's really awesome.

Content: Okay.

Content: This is fantastic.

Content: Gah! Nope.

Content_zh: 我看看，到底怎麼……

Content: Whoa...
Very cool.

Content: I want everyone to do that when you get your copy and fix it, okay?
Content_zh: 我希望大家拿到自己的那份之後，都這樣把它修好，好嗎？

Content: [Applause] That is kinda funny, we did Kickstart this thing
Content_zh: [掌聲] 這還滿好笑的，我們用 Kickstarter 發起了這東西，

Content: and now it's actually a box in my hand.
Content_zh: 現在它真的變成我手上的一個盒子了。

Content: Well, that pretty much ends this documentary.
Content_zh: 好，這部紀錄片差不多就到這裡了。

Content: And...scene.
Content_zh: 然後……收工。

Content: I really hoped when it came out people wouldn't forget about
Content_zh: 我真的希望它推出後，大家不要忘記

Content: how it was funded and just kinda talk about the game.
Content_zh: 它是怎麼募資的，而只是單純討論遊戲本身。

Content: But it seemed like every review had to talk about the development story,
Content_zh: 但似乎每篇評論都得提到開發故事，

Content: and so that was part of the lore for a long time, still.
Content_zh: 所以那很長一段時間都還是它傳說的一部分。

Content: So I hope, um, five years from now when people talk about adventure games,
Content_zh: 所以我希望，嗯，五年後當大家談到冒險遊戲時，

Content: they can look at it as just an adventure game.
Content_zh: 他們可以把它當作一個冒險遊戲

Content: And so I think it'll be remembered well, and people can just kinda get over
Content_zh: 所以我覺得它會被好好記住，而大家也可以慢慢放下

Content: the crowdfunding part of it.
Content_zh: 群眾募資的那一部分。

Content: Kipnis: I--to be honest with you, I'm not really sure how this whole thing is gonna be looked at in the end.
Content_zh: Kipnis：老實說，我不太確定這整件事最後會被怎麼看待。

Content: Um...
I--I guess, like, everyone has hopes for, like, a game when it comes out,
Content_zh: 我……我想，像是每個人對一款遊戲推出時都會有期待，

Content: and I had certain hopes that, um...
Content_zh: 而我也有某些期待，嗯……

Content: maybe were not fulfilled, necessarily.
Content_zh: 也許不一定有實現。

Content: And, uh, and it has more to do with just, like, I really hoped people would love the game.
Content_zh: 而且，呃，這比較像是……我真的希望大家會喜歡這款遊戲。

Content: Um, and it's not to say that they didn't, but I kinda just, um--
Content_zh: 嗯，這不是說他們不喜歡，但我只是，嗯……

Content: I think there--there was a lot of controversy surrounding the game and the company
Content_zh: 我想…… 關於遊戲和公司有很多爭議

Content: that I wish hadn't been there.
Content_zh: 我希望那些爭議沒有存在過。

Content: Like, I just wish it was...
Content_zh: 就像，我只是希望它是……

Content: I don't know.
Content_zh: 我不知道。

Content: Annable: It was a long, hard road, and it was very public,
Content_zh: Annable：這是一段漫長又艱難的路，而且非常公開，

Content: and I feel like there were a lot of things that changed.
Content_zh: 我覺得有很多事情改變了

Content: Um, and they weren't necessarily just because of "Broken Age" and its visibility.
Content_zh: 嗯，而且那些改變不一定只是因為《Broken Age》和它的能見度。

Content: I think the company is a different company now than it was three years ago,
Content_zh: 我覺得這家公司現在和三年前不同了

Content: and I'm trying to still figure out, why?
Content_zh: 我在試著弄清楚，為什麼？

Content: I don't know, are we just older?
Content_zh: 我不知道，我們是不是老了？

Content: Offscreen: Do you have any expectation of "Broken Age's" performance?
Content_zh: 畫外音：你對《Broken Age》的表現有什麼期待嗎？

Content: Annable: I don't know.
Content_zh: Annable：我不知道。

Content: Bailey: And then...uh, talking about sales of "Broken Age,"
Content_zh: Bailey：然後……呃，談到《Broken Age》的銷售，

Content: so, uh, sales on Steam, we had a feature spot.
Content_zh: Steam 上的銷售，我們有一個推薦版位。

Content: Um, it acted more like an update than, like, a--a new push.
Content_zh: 嗯，它比較像是一次更新，而不是一波新的推廣。

Content: Um, however, we got a feature on Apple, which is doing really well; about $25,000 a day.
Content_zh: 嗯，不過我們在 Apple 上有推薦，表現很好；一天大概有 25,000 美元。

Content: Um, and then we also have, um, sales for, uh, Sony.
Content_zh: 嗯，然後我們也有，呃，Sony 的銷售。

Content: PS4, we had, like, $100,000 worth of sales, uh, over the weekend.
Content_zh: PS4，我們週末大概有 10 萬美元的銷售額。

Content: And it just got featured, so, uh, that's cool.
Content_zh: 而且它剛被推薦，所以，呃，還不錯。

Content: Schafer: My expectation-- my expectation of "Broken Age" in the end
Content_zh: Schafer：我的期待……我對《Broken Age》最後的期待，

Content: would just be to break even.
Content_zh: 就只是能打平。

Content: I know it was kind of always like a Kickstarter, like, you plan on it--
Content_zh: 我知道它一直有點像 Kickstarter，你會照著計畫……

Content: Like, it's--the risk is gone of losing money on it, so you know you've broken even if you just make the game
Content_zh: 就是……賠錢的風險已經沒了，所以如果你只是用那筆錢做出遊戲，

Content: to that amount of money.
Content_zh: 你就知道已經打平了。

Content: But we made it, like, twice as much, almost, as we got in, or more.
Content_zh: 但我們做出來的規模，幾乎是拿到的錢的兩倍，甚至更多。

Content: And so, um, we will just about make that back.
Content_zh: 所以，嗯，我們差不多會把那筆錢賺回來。

Content: And the biggest change is that we don't need the publishers anymore, 100%.
Content_zh: 最大的變化是 我們不再需要發行商了 100%

Content: You know, it used to be, like, there was no money in the world outside of publishers.
Content_zh: 以前發行商外面沒有錢

Content: And I think crowdfunding's here to stay.
Content_zh: 我覺得群眾募資會繼續存在。

Content: I think it's, um, hopefully gonna grow.
Content_zh: 我覺得，嗯，希望它會成長。

Content: And in my dream-- my dream world, it would be big enough to fund AAA games
Content_zh: 在我的夢想中……在我的夢想世界裡，它會大到足以資助 AAA 遊戲，

Content: and it would be the way that we fund all games going forward.
Content_zh: 而且會成為我們未來資助所有遊戲的方式。

Content: So now when we're talking to a publisher, the deals are better.
Content_zh: 所以現在我們跟發行商談的時候，條件會更好。

Content: We're asking for less money, but we're also not entirely dependent on them
Content_zh: 我們要求少點錢 但我們也不完全靠他們

Content: to make payroll next week.
Content_zh: 來支付下週的薪水。

Content: We're not like, "Please, we just--sign us.
Content_zh: 我們不會像是：「拜託，我們只是……簽下我們吧。

Content: We'll--we'll sign anything, we just gotta make payroll." It's like, okay, you gotta kick us in the teeth
Content_zh: 我們……我們什麼都簽，我們只是得發薪水。」就像，好吧，你們每個月踹我們一腳，

Content: once a month and all this stuff.
Content_zh: 還有各種這類條件也行。

Content: And now we only have to take good deals with people we like.
Content_zh: 而現在我們只需要跟喜歡的人談好的合作。

Content: So we still work with publishers, but, um, it's different.
Content_zh: 所以我們仍然和發行商合作 但這不一樣

Content: Because we're 100% in control.
Content_zh: 因為我們百分之百掌握主導權。

Content: Annable: Yeah, we're not...
Content_zh: Annable：是啊，我們不是……

Content: we're not rollin' in millions of dollars.
Content_zh: 我們不是賺到幾百萬美元。

Content: I--I don't know if that would necessarily make us happier, though.
Content_zh: 我不知道那會不會讓我們更開心

Content: Schafer: You guys are the best.
Content_zh: Schafer：你們是最棒的

Content: Thank you for all your lovely audio.
Content_zh: 謝謝你們所有美好的音訊。

Content: And thanks for your beard.
Content_zh: 謝謝你的鬍子

Content: Stoddard: It's kind of a milestone.
Content_zh: Stoddard：這是個里程碑

Content: I think there's a-- there's a few things in general around gaming that "Broken Age," like,
Content_zh: 我覺得整體來說，遊戲界有幾件事是《Broken Age》

Content: really kinda did a push towards a new area.
Content_zh: 真的把大家推向了一個新領域。

Content: And I like that.
Content_zh: 我喜歡

Content: Um, there's a whole bunch of stuff, like, we could've done differently
Content_zh: 嗯，有一大堆事情，我們本來可以用不同方式做，

Content: and, like, we definitely fell down in some areas.
Content_zh: 而且我們確實在某些地方跌倒了。

Content: But, like, so what?
Content_zh: 但是，那又怎樣？

Content: Like, that's how you-- that's how you go forward.
Content_zh: 就是，這就是你……這就是你往前走的方式。

Content: Like, if you never fail, then you just aren't learning anything.
Content_zh: 如果你從不失敗，那你就什麼都學不到。

Content: You're just... being boring, really.
Content_zh: 你只是……真的很無聊而已。

Content: Franzke: I think it was a huge success.
Content_zh: Franzke：我想這是個很大的成功

Content: Without this project, who knows?
Content_zh: 沒有這個專案 誰知道呢

Content: Maybe Kickstarter would have never been the prime platform for, you know, crowdfunded games.
Content_zh: 也許 Kickstarter 就不會成為群眾募資遊戲的主要平台。

Content: I think it made it possible for a lot of other people to--to work on their dream projects,
Content_zh: 我覺得它讓很多其他人有機會去做自己的夢想專案，

Content: so that's--that's pretty cool, yeah.
Content_zh: 所以…… 這很酷

Content: So overall, you know, I couldn't be happier, I really couldn't.
Content_zh: 所以整體來說，我真的不能更開心了，真的。

Content: And, you know, the other day, um, the other day I went to just a random technology store
Content_zh: 然後，前幾天，嗯，我去了某間普通的科技商店，

Content: here in Germany, and--and we just, um...
Content_zh: 在德國 我們只是……

Content: walked by this little thing.
Content_zh: 走過這個小東西旁邊。

Content: So, just have--to see this physical manifestation of all the work that, you know,
Content_zh: 所以，能看到所有投入其中的心血，

Content: went into--into this game is, like, super awesome.
Content_zh: 以實體形式呈現在眼前，真的超棒。

Content: Schafer: Hey, Ray! Good job. Crook: Has the soundtrack in there.
Content_zh: Schafer：嘿，Ray！幹得好。Crook：裡面有原聲帶。

Content: Schafer: Oh, I forgot to mention, it has the soundtrack in there.
Content_zh: Schafer：喔，我忘了提，裡面有原聲帶。

Content: Crook: I mean, I guess we did accomplish what we wanted to, which was to do this game.
Content_zh: Crook：我是說，我想我們確實完成了想做的事，也就是做出這款遊戲。

Content: And we--and we did that, and we--and we finished it.
Content_zh: 我們……我們做到了，而且我們……我們完成了它。

Content: I think what we did do that was the-- that I think was the most beneficial to everybody
Content_zh: 我覺得我們真正做的事，也是我覺得對大家最有幫助的事，

Content: was that we kind of cracked things open and we let people look inside of how things work.
Content_zh: 是我們把東西稍微拆開，讓大家看到事情內部是怎麼運作的。

Content: And this maybe will help them to see how things-- uh, how difficult, uh, it is,
Content_zh: 這也許能幫助他們看見事情……呃，看見這有多困難，

Content: and how we're kinda lucky that anything even comes out.
Content_zh: 以及任何東西最後能推出，其實我們都有點幸運。

Content: Like, that any video games actually get finished, um, is kind of a miracle.
Content_zh: 任何電子遊戲真的能完成，嗯，某種程度上都是奇蹟。

Content: And--and so that's good.
Content_zh: 所以…… 那很好

Content: I hope that-- that helps...
Content_zh: 我希望…… 那有幫助……

Content: I--I hope that helps people who are fans of video games and like playing video games,
Content_zh: 我希望這能幫助那些喜歡玩電子遊戲的人

Content: it just helps them understand and have a little bit of empathy towards, uh,
Content_zh: 只是幫助他們理解，並對，呃，

Content: those of us who are in this industry.
Content_zh: 我們這些在這個產業裡的人多一點同理心。

Content: Um...you know, we're lucky to be in it, and I--you know, I'm grateful to be in it, um,
Content_zh: 我們很幸運能參與其中 我很感激能參與其中

Content: but it's not always easy, and--and it's-- and I'm glad that
Content_zh: 但這並不總是容易，而且…… 我很高興

Content: we were able to show that to people in--in an honest way, so it's kinda neat.
Content_zh: 我們能用誠實的方式把這件事展現給大家，所以還滿不錯的。

Content: I mean, if Double Fine's not around in 10 years, let's just, say, um-- it'll always be rem--
Content_zh: 我是說，如果 Double Fine 十年後不在了，那就，嗯……它永遠會被記……

Content: like, it's out there now forever, right?
Content_zh: 就是，它現在已經永遠存在於那裡了，對吧？

Content: Once it's on the Internet, um, it'll always be there.
Content_zh: 一旦上網，就永遠存在。

Content: And so...so...
Content_zh: 所以……所以……

Content: Double Fine will always be...
Content_zh: Double Fine 會永遠……

Content: ...around.
Content_zh: ……存在。

Content: [Music] Stoddard: I think it was very positive.
Content_zh: [音樂] Stoddard：我覺得這非常正面。

Content: I think there was a lot of stuff that we really did contribute
Content_zh: 我覺得我們真的為遊戲產業

Content: to the games industry.
Content_zh: 貢獻了很多東西。

Content: And...uh, and there's a lot of stuff that we sidestepped and, like, misstepped.
Content_zh: 而且……呃，也有很多事情我們避開了，或是走錯了。

Content: And we're trying to learn from that, too.
Content_zh: 我們也想從中學習。

Content: So, um, considering all that, I--going back and playing the actual game,
Content_zh: 所以，嗯，考慮到這一切，我……回頭去玩實際的遊戲時，

Content: I actually think we did really solid work.
Content_zh: 其實我覺得我們做得很扎實。

Content: And I actually think it's, um...
Content_zh: 其實我覺得……

Content: I think it's a really good game.
Content_zh: 我覺得這是個很棒的遊戲。

Content: I haven't really seen many reactions that are just blasé.
Content_zh: 我其實沒看到太多只是冷淡無感的反應。

Content: Like, it--it's usually a strong reaction, one way or the other.
Content_zh: 通常都是很強烈的反應，不是這邊就是那邊。

Content: And to me, in any art form, that's great.
Content_zh: 對我來說，在任何藝術形式裡，這都很棒。

Content: Like, that means that it's provoking something.
Content_zh: 這代表它激起了某種東西。

Content: And I think that's what this is about.
Content_zh: 我覺得這就是重點。

Content: If, like, we did three years of work and there was all these trials and tribulations
Content_zh: 如果我們做了三年，經歷了這麼多考驗和磨難，

Content: and at the end somebody just went, "Meh," like, that would be crushing!
Content_zh: 結果最後有人只是說：「嗯哼。」那真的會很打擊！

Content: But it hasn't been like that; it's been the opposite.
Content_zh: 但情況不是那樣；反而完全相反。

Content: Like, it's either really, really good or really, really awful or, you know,
Content_zh: 不是非常、非常好，就是非常、非常糟，或者，你知道，

Content: very, very vocal, no matter what.
Content_zh: 不管怎樣，大家都非常有意見。

Content: Great.
Content_zh: 太好了。

Content: Then we did our job.
Content_zh: 那我們就完成了自己的工作。

Content: [Music]
Content_zh: [音樂]
